TokenUsage: local token usage dashboard for Codex and Claude Code

TokenUsage helps Windows users measure local token usage across Codex and Claude Code in one dashboard. It focuses on persisted history, model visibility, local-first operation, and desktop widgets for quick monitoring.

Install from GitHub

git clone https://github.com/Aniian709/TokenUsage.git

Then run npm install in the project root and start the local dashboard with node .\bin\tracker.js serve.

What TokenUsage provides

  • Unified local usage tracking for Codex and Claude Code.
  • Persisted historical snapshots even after some original session files are deleted.
  • Structured visibility into model, daily, 7-day, 30-day, and all-time consumption.
  • Windows desktop widgets controlled from the dashboard.

Who should use TokenUsage

TokenUsage is designed for developers who use Codex and Claude Code on Windows and want one local dashboard to understand token consumption without relying on a cloud account or online sync.

It is especially useful if you want to compare models, inspect recent usage windows, keep local history after deleting some chats, and pin always-on-top usage widgets to the Windows desktop.

The repository includes prebuilt frontend assets so other users can clone it and start using it directly without first rebuilding the dashboard.

Frequently asked questions

How do I install TokenUsage?

Clone the GitHub repository, run npm install, then start it with node .\bin\tracker.js serve.

Which AI coding clients are supported?

TokenUsage focuses on Codex and Claude Code local usage tracking.

What does TokenUsage track?

It tracks local token usage, daily detail, heatmap activity, model breakdown, trend views, and usage limits.
